div1.entryFree: Notable, excellent, remarkable, egregious (oftener in a bad than in a good sense): my master is become a n. lover, Gent. II, 5, 44. a n. lubber, Gent. II, 5, 44 we shall find this friar a n. fellow, Meas. V, 268. thou wilt prove a n. argument, Ado I, 1, 258. he's a most n. coward, All's III, 6, 10. on that vice in him will my revenge find n. cause to work, Tw. II, 3, 166. come by some n. shame, II, 5, 6. turn him into a n. contempt, II, 5, 6 set upon Aguecheek a n. report of valour, III, 4, 210. n. pirate, V, 72. a n. passion of wonder appeared in them, Wint. V, 2, 17. you depend upon a n. gentleman, Troil. III, 1, 6 (Ff and M. Edd. noble). the fleers, the gibes, and n. scorns, Oth. IV, 1, 83. O n. strumpet, V, 1, 78.
